_meta.note: Compiled from named analyst articles for 2026 NFL Draft pick reasoning. Quotes trimmed to <=240 chars for UI.

LV.text: Mendoza has built his reputation on elite processing, composure, and precision in the short-to-intermediate game after leading Indiana to a historic 16-0 national championship.
The Raiders' non-negotiables are a level of mental and physical toughness and a passion for the game — and Mendoza checks every box the College Scouting Director wants.
Vegas paved the way for Mendoza to start Day 1 — trading Geno Smith, signing Tyler Linderbaum, and pairing him with Klint Kubiak as play caller plus Cousins and Brady as mentors.

PIT.text: Vegas paved the way for Mendoza to start Day 1 — trading Geno Smith, signing Tyler Linderbaum, and pairing him with Klint Kubiak as play caller plus Cousins and Brady as mentors.

NYJ.text: Rob Rang: Mendoza isn't the no-brainer franchise QB that leads off a draft class, but his prototypical size, mobility, lightning-quick release and leadership make him this year's surest thing at the game's most important position.
Mendoza has spoken with the Jets, Cardinals and Browns — the teams at No. 2, 3 and 6 that could be in the market for a new franchise QB behind Vegas.

ARI.text: The Cardinals draft their quarterback of the future in Mendoza — a rhythm passer with pinpoint accuracy and touch whose mistake-free football and clutch playmaking fit what a QB-needy Arizona roster desperately needs.
Mendoza became an option atop a weak QB class for QB-needy teams behind the Raiders, with the Cardinals at No. 3 firmly in the mix after their meetings with the Heisman winner.

NYJ.text: Matt Miller: I have the Jets opting for the upside of Reese over the proven production of David Bailey. Reese has double-digit sack potential and can be a versatile piece for Aaron Glenn.
At 6'4" and 241 pounds, Reese's Micah Parsons-style versatility is why the Jets view him as the cornerstone of Aaron Glenn's hybrid front at No. 2.

ARI.text: Reese's 4.47 at 241 pounds with 32½" arms checks virtually every box NFL teams covet — Arizona finished 28th in sack rate, and his flexibility as off-ball LB and edge is what Cardinals' front seven desperately needs.
Reese poses a challenging decision for the Cardinals at No. 3 — he's one of three edge defenders in play alongside David Bailey and Rueben Bain if that's the direction Arizona targets.

LV.text: Reese and Sonny Styles headline the best linebacker class in years — a Raiders front that lacks Maxx Crosby support could pair Reese's edge versatility with Crosby to transform the pass rush.
Raiders predicted to pair Maxx Crosby with Arvell Reese — Reese's athletic profile and double-digit sack ceiling give Vegas a second true rush threat opposite Crosby.

general_quotes.text: Matt Holder: Reese is the most physically impressive linebacker in the 2026 draft class — rare athletic profile to rush the edge while keeping fluid hips to drop into coverage.

ARI.text: Tony Pauline: The Cardinals' GM 'wants' Texas Tech edge rusher David Bailey. If he's no longer available, the front office will 'work hard' to trade down in the first round.
Arizona finished 29th in PFF pass-rush grade and 28th in sack rate — Bailey's 14.5 sacks and elite get-off are exactly the fix Jonathan Gannon's defense has been missing.

NYJ.text: Matt Miller: Texas Tech edge David Bailey is the 'perfect fit' for the Jets at No. 2 — the best pure edge rusher in the class with FBS-leading 14.5 sacks and a 21.3% pressure rate.
The addition of 365-pound nose tackle T'Vondre Sweat makes Bailey a more palatable pick — he can be aggressive off the edge without killing the structure of Aaron Glenn's defense.

LV.text: Bailey is considered by many league observers the best pure edge rusher in the 2026 draft — a one-year Texas Tech transfer whose 81 pressures and 19.5 TFLs translate anywhere, including as Maxx Crosby's bookend.

general_quotes.text: Bailey's speed-to-power rushes combined with upper-body strength give him a bull rush that takes tackles by surprise — making him a tough rusher to defend at the next level.

TEN.text: Titans GM Mike Borgonzi: 'I think he's a special player' — praising Love's speed and ability to do things out of the backfield as a receiver.
Mike Tannenbaum: Love is 'too unique to pass up' for the Titans or Giants in the top 10 — the kind of three-down, home-run runner that transforms a club's rushing attack.

NYG.text: Mike Tannenbaum: Love is 'too unique to pass up' for the Titans or Giants in the top 10 — the kind of three-down, home-run runner that transforms a club's rushing attack.

ARI.text: There is growing chatter in league circles that Notre Dame RB Jeremiyah Love is in play with Arizona at No. 3 — he could become the first running back drafted in the top five since 2018.
Love piled up 2,882 yards and 36 rushing TDs at Notre Dame — 6.9 yards per carry and a 93.7 PFF rushing grade make him the top-three talent Arizona can't ignore if they keep the pick.

LAR.text: Conor Orr: The Rams make a massive move into the top five, sending picks 13, 61, 93 and a 2027 first to Tennessee for No. 4 — pairing Love with Puka Nacua and Davante Adams makes LA an instant Super Bowl frontrunner.
Sean McVay could make sweet music with Love — the most talented back he'd have since 2017 OPOY Todd Gurley. If Love gets past Washington at 7, LA could call to acquire the draft's best player.

general_quotes.text: Love is a three-down back with elite playing speed — his 4.36 forty at the Combine confirmed he's a big-play creator who belongs in the top 10.

NYG.text: Albert Breer: Harbaugh has a history with battleship-commander middle linebackers — Ray Lewis, C.J. Mosley, Roquan Smith — and sees Styles fitting that role in the Giants defense.
Styles is the prototype modern hybrid defender — a former safety at 6'5"/245 with a 4.46 forty who Harbaugh can use as a chess piece like Kyle Hamilton in Baltimore.

BAL.text: Styles is the prototype modern hybrid defender — a former safety at 6'5"/245 with a 4.46 forty who Harbaugh can use as a chess piece like Kyle Hamilton in Baltimore.

KC.text: Styles posted elite coverage grades and elite run-defense grades in 2025 with just a 2 percent missed-tackle rate — range, length, and versatility at every level.

TEN.text: Under new HC Robert Saleh, Tennessee is building a defensive identity around speed, versatility, and disruption — Styles can rush off the edge or play hybrid LB/S in sub-packages, fixing the Titans' biggest weaknesses.
Titans take Styles at No. 4 — with Cam Ward already in place, Saleh pairs him with a rare blend of intelligence, 6'5" size, 80⅞" wingspan, 4.46 speed and record-setting combine athleticism.

DAL.text: Eric Edholm: Cowboys make a bold choice at LB — Styles is one of the best prospects in the entire class at a position of dire need for Dallas, with Jerry Jones possibly having to move up from 12 to secure him.

WAS.text: Todd McShay: Styles is the player Dan Quinn wants most if he's available at No. 7 — 'Styles is going seven to Washington if he's on the board.'
Styles' versatility, physicality and ability to play multiple defensive roles fit exactly the fast, aggressive identity Quinn wants — he can play LB, box, space, and set the physical tone.
Mina Kimes: 'Carnell Tate would be such a great pairing with Terry McLaurin. I think, at minimum, he is an excellent 1B. I love him with McLaurin.'
Todd McShay: The Commanders are 'absolutely' in on Ohio State's Carnell Tate over Jordyn Tyson if he's on the board at No. 7 — they prefer his clean injury profile.
Jeremy Fowler: Tate is considered the 'safest' receiver prospect this year, with landing spots ranging from the Titans at No. 4 to the Commanders at seven.

TEN.text: Jeremy Fowler: Tate is considered the 'safest' receiver prospect this year, with landing spots ranging from the Titans at No. 4 to the Commanders at seven.

NYG.text: Tate is a long, savvy, competitive, versatile WR at 6'2¾" with 31¾" arms — a 90.2 PFF grade with zero drops and 12-of-14 contested catches. A starting-caliber pairing for Jaxson Dart at No. 5.
Adding Tate would provide immediate help for Dart in his second season — with young playmakers returning to full strength, New York would have a dynamic young core at the skill positions.

NO.text: Jordan Reid: The Saints would 'quickly' select Ohio State WR Carnell Tate with the 8th overall pick if he's still available — the most-mocked player to New Orleans this week.
Adding Tate to pair with Chris Olave gives Tyler Shough a true outside 14-TD receiver as the Saints evaluate him as the long-term answer — a 90.3 PFF grade (sixth of 679 qualified WRs) confirms it.

ARI.text: Tyson is a twitchy, dynamic playmaker with route-running traits to be an impact matchup receiver — arguably the best pure route runner in the class.

NYG.text: Talent-wise Tyson is the WR1 of this class, but an ACL/MCL/PCL in 2022, a 2024 broken collarbone, and 2025 hamstring make teams cautious — he could slide to the late teens.

NO.text: Tyson leads off a 7-round Saints mock — 3rd-Team AP All-American with 75 catches, 1,101 yards, 10 TDs in 2024; teams prefer him as the most well-rounded, pro-ready WR in the draft.
Daniel Jeremiah has Tyson at No. 17 overall and Bucky Brooks ranks him the top WR — an advanced release package and route tempo that make him the most pro-ready receiver for the Saints at 8.

NYJ.text: Tyson has long been viewed as a likely candidate for the Jets' 16th pick — but scouts say it feels more unlikely he'll still be available as his stock rises into draft week.

KC.text: Mel Kiper Jr. and Pete Schrager both predicted the Chiefs take Tyson — pairing him with Rashee Rice, Xavier Worthy and Travis Kelce rounds out KC's passing attack as Mahomes returns from injury.
Former NFL executive on Tyson to Chiefs: 'Perfect for them' — 23 TDs at Arizona State with the release package and route nuance KC's offense can weaponize on Day 1.

general_quotes.text: Tyson subtly alters his pathing and bends stems to find voids in zone and beat man — quick in and out of breaks with the long speed to generate explosive plays.

NO.text: Downs split snaps between free safety (920), box (865), and slot (574) — the positionless chess piece Saints DC Brandon Staley wants as the back end's signal caller.
Downs allowed just 92 yards after the catch and zero coverage TDs last season while earning Big Ten Defensive Player of the Year — a plug-and-play safety for New Orleans at 8.

NYG.text: Downs split snaps between free safety (920), box (865), and slot (574) — the positionless chess piece Saints DC Brandon Staley wants as the back end's signal caller.

PHI.text: Safety is one of the most urgent needs on the Eagles roster — Downs has outstanding awareness of play development and an attacking style closing on the line in the run game.
Barrett Brooks breaks down film on Caleb Downs and analyzes why he would be a good fit for the Eagles — the alpha signal-caller Howie Roseman needs in the back end.

PIT.text: If Downs falls out of the top 10, he could fascinate the Steelers — his instincts and football savvy would probably pay off for the franchise for years to come, as long as the medicals check out.

general_quotes.text: Daniel Jeremiah has Downs at No. 9 overall and Bucky Brooks has him as his top safety — a three-year starter and alpha who brings immense juice on every snap.

KC.text: The Chiefs are the most likely landing spot for Bain — Kansas City needs pass-rush help and Bain fits exactly what Steve Spagnuolo values: power, hand usage, and effort.

MIA.text: The Chiefs are the most likely landing spot for Bain — Kansas City needs pass-rush help and Bain fits exactly what Steve Spagnuolo values: power, hand usage, and effort.

GB.text: Pete Dougherty: Bain is one of at least five edges who has 'little chance of making it even near No. 52' for Green Bay — but if Gutekunst breaks from type and trades up, Bain is the kind of power rusher Packers covet.

CIN.text: Fields Yates predicts the Bengals select edge rusher Rueben Bain Jr. with the 10th overall pick — loose hips, violent hands, power-speed blend and 9.5 sacks in 2025 make him an immediate disruptor.
The Bengals are locking in a pre-draft visit with projected top-10 pick Rueben Bain Jr. — Cincinnati needs the pass-rush reinforcement and Bain's Ted Hendricks pedigree fits.

DAL.text: Bain's power-based game would give new DC Christian Parker a front-seven tone-setter who can rush outside and inside — the second-best pure edge rusher in the class and a steal at No. 12.
Jordan Reid: Bain is one of two players 'believed' to be on Dallas' radar in a trade-up scenario — Jerry Jones' dream pick for a Cowboys defense that needs more bite.

LV.text: Mike Band mock 2.0 has Bain sliding past Vegas despite fit — his 92.8 PFF grade (second among P4 edges only to Bailey) gives the Raiders rush insurance if Mendoza isn't the pick at 1.

general_quotes.text: Bain might not be built like a prototypical edge rusher, but he plays like a potential top-10 pick — destructive with his power and hands and a rare run-defending presence.
One coach told Yardbarker Bain's arm length 'does scare them,' especially against long tackles — the sub-33" arms are why some boards have him slipping out of the top 15.

MIA.text: Mel Kiper Jr. ranks Mauigoa as the No. 1 offensive tackle in the class — raw talent, speed, lateral movement, and quickness that labels him an elite top-five prospect.
Chris Simms: Mauigoa is a tone-setter with the physical mentality O-line coaches dream of installing — anchor and power that translate to the NFL on Day 1.
At 6'5½" and 329 pounds with elite hand strength, Mauigoa's Browns/Dolphins fit is about immediate LT stability — how far he'd slide is the question, not whether he starts Day 1.

CLE.text: At 6'5½" and 329 pounds with elite hand strength, Mauigoa's Browns/Dolphins fit is about immediate LT stability — how far he'd slide is the question, not whether he starts Day 1.

DET.text: At 6'5½" and 329 pounds with elite hand strength, Mauigoa's Browns/Dolphins fit is about immediate LT stability — how far he'd slide is the question, not whether he starts Day 1.

NYG.text: Mel Kiper final mock: Giants take Mauigoa at No. 10 — a consensus All-American and three-year RT starter who allowed just 15 pressures and 2 sacks in 2025 and lifts the line to the next level.
With the Giants' No. 10 pick acquired from the Dexter Lawrence trade, they'd be happy to see Mauigoa still on the board — he'd take the offensive line unit to the next level.

ARI.text: Right tackle is the most pressing void for Arizona entering the draft — Mauigoa allowed only two sacks during Miami's run to the national title game and would be an immediate replacement opposite Paris Johnson Jr.

Mauigoa is largely regarded as the best tackle prospect in the draft and has often been projected to the Cardinals at No. 3 — fierce run blocker with 33¼" arms and an AP First-Team All-American resume.

BAL.text: Mauigoa told teams he's willing to move from tackle to guard if that's the path to the field — fitting Baltimore's guard need and giving the Ravens elite versatility at No. 14.
Mauigoa is a potential first-round pick for the Ravens who says he's willing to play guard — his Miami resume and physical tone fit Baltimore's AFC North trench demands.
